steps.title: Spot the temporal shift
steps.content: The past perfect "had served" indicates that Massey's presidency of the Black Physicists organization occurred before his 1990 NSF appointment—the passage shifts backward in time.

steps.title: Match the chronological relationship
steps.content:
The logical relationship is sequence (backward). The passage goes from Massey's 1990 NSF appointment back to an earlier role, so the blank must signal a prior event.
  • Choice A — In conclusion,: signals a final summary, not a shift backward in time, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — Previously,: explicitly marks an event that occurred before the 1990 appointment, matching "had served," so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — Thus,: signals cause-effect, implying the NSF appointment caused the Black Physicists presidency — but the presidency came first, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — For example,: signals a specific illustration of a general claim, but the prior presidency is not an example of the NSF appointment, so fails. ✗
That is the correct answer.
